title: CAWS Command-Safety Adapter Sidecar
description: User-extensible tool->facet adapter table for the command-safety classifier hook. STRICTLY BOUNDED: a row maps a tool executable + subcommand/resource-alias to an ABSTRACT FACET assignment only. It CANNOT define policy outcomes (allow/ask/deny), override the lattice, or introduce new capability kinds or facet values -- those are governed by docs/architecture/command-capability-taxonomy.md + the template, and require a CAWS spec to change. A document that violates this schema FAILS CLOSED in the loader (sidecar ignored, diagnostic ask surfaced); it is never silently trusted. additionalProperties:false throughout is the structural enforcement of the authority boundary.

version.description: Sidecar schema version. Only 1 is recognized; any other value fails closed.

adapters.description: Map of tool executable basename -> adapter spec. The executable is matched after wrapper-peel and absolute-path basename resolution (so '/usr/bin/kubectl' matches the 'kubectl' key).

resource_aliases.description: Resource short-name -> canonical name, expanded before subcommand-path matching (e.g. {"po":"pod","ns":"namespace"}). Aliases ONLY; cannot carry facets.

unknown_subcommand.description: Facet behavior for a subcommand not listed below. Default 'no_escalation' = engine intent-fallback + amplifier flags still apply, but tool identity adds nothing. This is the ONLY two-value knob and neither value is a decision outcome.

subcommands.description: List of subcommand-path -> facet-assignment rows. First matching path wins; '*' is a single-token wildcard.

path.description: Subcommand path as a list of positional tokens, e.g. ["delete"], ["system","prune"], ["delete","*"]. '*' matches any single token.

facet_assignment.description: Abstract facet assignment. ALL values are closed enums from the governed taxonomy. There is deliberately NO 'decision'/'allow'/'deny'/'policy' key -- the lattice owns the decision; the sidecar only describes WHAT the command does, never WHAT TO DO about it.

trace_label.description: Optional non-authoritative concrete label for debug output only (e.g. "K8S_DELETE_BROAD"). Never consulted by any decision branch.
